subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Building a Weather Kit
description: One-minute skill: Building a Weather Kit.

hook.text: 72 hours. That's how long you may be on your own after a major weather disaster before help arrives.

buildup.text: A weather emergency kit provides water, food, warmth, light, and communication for at least 3 days.

discovery.text: The essentials: 1 gallon of water per person per day, non-perishable food, flashlight, and batteries.

twist.text: Most people who own emergency kits haven't updated them in years—expired food and dead batteries.

climax.text: Checking and rotating your kit every 6 months keeps it ready when the emergency hits.

punchline.text: 72 hours alone. One kit. Check it twice a year. That's the difference.

quiz.question: How much water should an emergency kit contain per person per day?
